syntaxAssertions.description: asterisk thematic break resolves as a horizontal rule
plus signs alone are paragraph text, not thematic breaks
equals signs alone are paragraph text, not thematic breaks
four-space indented stars are an indented code block, not a thematic break
spaced dash markers form a thematic break
thematic break can appear as the block content of a list item

markdown:
# foo
## foo
### foo
#### foo
##### foo
###### foo

syntaxAssertions.description: heading text resolves through heading text and inline text nodes
emphasis inside heading text keeps a nested inline emphasis path
hashes not preceded by spaces remain heading text
escaped closing ATX marker characters stay in heading text

markdown:
~~~~    ruby startline=3 $%@#$
def foo(x)
  return 3
end
~~~~~~~

syntaxAssertions.description: CommonMark type 6 HTML blocks can interrupt after paragraph text
the preceding line remains a normal paragraph before the interrupting HTML block
CommonMark table HTML blocks preserve their raw block until the blank-line boundary
content after a blank line following an HTML table block becomes a paragraph

markdown:
import Text.HTML.TagSoup

main :: IO ()
main = print $ parseTags tags
okay
html:
import Text.HTML.TagSoup

main :: IO ()
main = print $ parseTags tags

okay

syntaxAssertions.description: CommonMark type 1 pre blocks continue through blank lines until their matching end tag
text after the pre end tag starts a normal paragraph

markdown:
<script type="text/javascript">
// JavaScript example

document.getElementById("demo").innerHTML = "Hello JavaScript!";
</script>
okay

syntaxAssertions.description: CommonMark type 1 script blocks continue through blank lines until their matching end tag
raw HTML inside a blockquote keeps the quote boundary before the following paragraph
the unquoted line after the blank line starts a normal paragraph
CommonMark HTML comments continue through blank lines until the closing comment marker
CommonMark processing-instruction HTML blocks ignore inner greater-than characters until ?>
CommonMark declaration HTML blocks expose declaration marker and body tokens

markdown:
<![CDATA[
function matchwo(a,b)
{
  if (a < b && a < 0) then {
    return 1;

  } else {

    return 0;
  }
}
]]>
okay
html: okay

syntaxAssertions.description: CommonMark CDATA HTML blocks continue through nested blank lines until ]]>
blank lines around type 6 HTML block tags allow Markdown paragraphs between raw tags
the closing HTML tag remains a source-addressable raw HTML block
CommonMark table HTML blocks split at blank lines while preserving raw body source mapping
multiline reference definitions keep the continued destination span
resolved shortcut references still produce inline link content after the definition
angle-wrapped multiline destinations preserve the url syntax node on the continuation line
reference labels resolve case-insensitively for shortcut links
non-ASCII reference destinations stay attached to the original definition syntax span
multiline reference labels keep separator markers addressable when the closing bracket starts a line
later definitions can still carry multiline titles in the original syntax tree
resolved references from multiple definitions stay merged in one paragraph
multiline reference labels keep a cross-line label span in the original syntax tree
resolved full reference links still map their text inside the later paragraph
unicode case folding resolves shortcut reference text to the later definition
the original definition label span still points at the unfolded label text

description: unresolved reference labels reprocess backslash escapes as literal inline text

syntaxAssertions.description: invalid inline link syntax still falls back to a shortcut reference link for the leading label
the trailing parenthesized text remains literal paragraph text after the shortcut reference
unmatched reference-like syntax leaves the leading label literal instead of consuming the later matching reference
the later matching full reference still resolves from the second bracket pair
standalone image lines stay inside paragraphs for the CommonMark profile
image alt syntax keeps the raw markdown label in the syntax tree even when html alt text is flattened
blockquote heading content keeps nested quote heading path
blockquote paragraph content stays merged across lazy lines

markdown:
A paragraph
with two lines.

    indented code

> A block quote.
html:
A paragraph with two lines.

indented code

A block quote.

markdown:
1.  A paragraph
    with two lines.

        indented code

    > A block quote.
html:
1. A paragraph with two lines.

    indented code

    A block quote.

syntaxAssertions.description: list paragraph content is nested under ordered list item
indented code remains a nested code block inside the list item
nested block quote content stays under the same list item
wide post-marker padding keeps a deep enough continuation line inside the list item paragraph
blank-start list items still map paragraph content under the same list item

1.  A paragraph
     with two lines.

         indented code
     > A block quote.

1. A paragraph with two lines.

    indented code

    A block quote.

      with two lines.

          indented code

      > A block quote.

1. A paragraph with two lines.

    indented code

    A block quote.

       with two lines.

           indented code

       > A block quote.

with two lines.

          indented code

      > A block quote.

    indented code

syntaxAssertions.description: shallow marker indentation keeps sibling list items at the same level
a nested list can be the first block inside a list item
a nested ordered list can be the first block inside a nested unordered list item

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the final syntax tree keeps nested list-first item paths searchable

syntaxAssertions.description: an atx heading can be the first block inside a list item
a bullet list can interrupt a paragraph without a blank line

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the final syntax tree keeps paragraph-interrupting bullet list items searchable

markdown:
The number of windows in my house is
14.  The number of doors is 6.
html: The number of windows in my house is 14. The number of doors is 6.

markdown:
The number of windows in my house is
1.  The number of doors is 6.
html:
The number of windows in my house is

  1. The number of doors is 6.

syntaxAssertions.description: only ordered lists starting with 1 interrupt paragraphs
nested loose sublists keep the second paragraph inside the deepest list item

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the final syntax tree preserves deep loose-list paragraph paths

markdown:
- a
 - b
  - c
   - d
  - e
 - f
- g

syntaxAssertions.description: a blank line between block elements keeps the second paragraph inside the same loose list item
the visible paragraph text stays under the same list item that owns the hidden definition
reference definition opening markers inside list items remain addressable as hidden block children in the original tree

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the visible paragraph text stays under the same list item after the final syntax tree rebuild
reference definition opening markers inside list items remain addressable as hidden block children after the final tree rebuild

syntaxAssertions.description: a block-first fenced code list item exposes code content directly under the same list item

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the rebuilt tree keeps fenced code content under the block-first list item

syntaxAssertions.description: a loose sublist does not force the outer list item to become paragraph-wrapped

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the final syntax tree keeps the loose sublist paragraph under the nested list item

syntaxAssertions.description: blockquote content inside a tight list item remains reachable through the quote and paragraph path

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the rebuilt syntax tree keeps quoted paragraph content under the same list item

syntaxAssertions.description: mixed quote-plus-code list items expose the quoted paragraph path in the original syntax tree

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the rebuilt syntax tree keeps the code content under the sibling code block after a quote

syntaxAssertions.description: the block-leading fenced code item keeps its code content path in the original tree
the paragraph after a block-leading fenced code block stays under the same list item

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the rebuilt tree keeps the block-leading fenced code content under the same list item
the rebuilt syntax tree keeps the post-fence paragraph attached to the same list item

syntaxAssertions.description: a loose outer item keeps its nested sublist reachable without losing the inner tight item path
the trailing paragraph stays attached to the same outer loose list item

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: the rebuilt tree keeps the inner tight sublist item under the loose outer item
the rebuilt tree keeps the trailing paragraph on the outer loose item

markdown:
- a
  - b
  - c

- d
  - e
  - f

syntaxAssertions.description: a loose outer list can still keep its nested sublists tight

finalSyntaxAssertions.description: a loose outer list still keeps the first nested sublist item tight in the rebuilt tree
the rebuilt tree preserves the second outer list item and its nested tight sublist

syntaxAssertions.description: multi-backtick code spans can contain single backticks and keep the full source span
underscore emphasis produces a normal inline emphasis node
punctuation before an underscore opener still allows CommonMark emphasis
inline links retain label, target, and title syntax children
angle autolinks produce an inline link target path

markdown:
\*not emphasized* \
not a tag \[not a link](/foo) \`not code` 1\. not a list \* not a list \# not a heading \[foo]: /url "not a reference" \ö not a character entity
html: *not emphasized* <br/> not a tag [not a link](/foo) `not code` 1. not a list * not a list # not a heading [foo]: /url "not a reference" ö not a character entity

syntaxAssertions.description: escaped reference-looking syntax remains paragraph text instead of becoming a definition
escaped opening emphasis delimiter remains literal text
